user_report: I set the subscribe button's bottom margin to 8px but it still has a huge gap under it.
The nav links should be blue per my stylesheet but they render grey.
The maintenance alert stays bright red even though I overrode its background with a more specific rule.
The headline should be dark navy per the stylesheet but it renders grey.
The fine print under the sale banner is red and I never styled that paragraph at all.
The gap between the intro text and the list is way bigger than the 12px top margin I set on the list.
notes: Collapsed sibling margins: the pass criterion is attributing the 48px margin that actually produces the gap. The engine has no explicit margin-collapse diagnostic (v0.x); if one lands, tighten these markers.

user_report: The promo banner sticks out of its container on the right.
My chart has width:100% but it never gets wider than about 600px.
The first cell in my flex row refuses to shrink and the row overflows its 480px width.
I put justify-content: space-between on the toolbar but the buttons stay bunched to the left.
expected: only affects flex or grid containers

user_report: My tooltip has z-index 9999 but it still slides under the site header.
expected: z-index:9999 is scoped inside context created by

user_report: The nav is position:sticky but it just scrolls away with the page instead of sticking.
The signup button is not on the page at all.
The buy button is visible but clicking it does nothing.
The "New" badge on the product card never shows up.
The hero section has completely vanished and the content below moved up.
The saved-changes notice shows up as a green bar with no text in it.
My theme's blue call-to-action button turned red after I installed the popup plugin.
Footer links render orange even though the theme stylesheet says they should be blue.
I gave section headings a 24px bottom margin but there is no space under them anymore.
The hide sidebar button animation does not work properly, it is not hiding the sidebar smoothly.
notes: Flagship v0.3 case (SPEC §14.5): the timeline must name the CANCELLED width transition, the display change that killed it, and the handler file. Served over http because LoAF script attribution is empty on file:// pages. The kill is on a 100ms timeout so the transition genuinely STARTS before dying (a same-task kill never reaches animationStarted — CDP then carries no target for the cancelled animation and the verdict degrades to the weaker 'never started' line).

user_report: Clicking the promo banner's close button does nothing.
notes: The honest absence IS the diagnosis: the handler sits on the hidden .close-icon sibling (js/case22-banner.js), so the right element has no click listener and includeAncestors (default) surfaces nothing relevant up the chain — 'none for click' proves the element-level absence, 'none up the chain' proves ancestors/document/window were checked too.

user_report: My card should fade in but it just pops.
notes: Seeds the R4 finding (SPEC §14.3): `transition: opacity` declares the property but leaves transition-duration at its 0s default, so the change is instant by design. args.property carries the user's expectation ('should fade in' → opacity), which is what arms the R4 check. Empirically verified: computed transition-property=opacity, transition-duration=0s, and getAnimations() stays empty (zero-duration transitions never run), so the declared-half analysis must carry the case.

user_report: The close button's × looks off-center — a bit too far to the left — even though the button is a perfect square with no padding.
notes: The box model is symmetric (32x32, no padding asymmetry) so no box tool can see the offset; the glyph is pushed off-center by text-align:left + text-indent, an ink-relative offset. measure_element reports a non-zero horizontal ink-vs-content-box-center delta (empirically -8.3px for × in 16px Arial here) and a 'shift content right' fix hint. Markers assert the non-zero horizontal offset direction + fix hint without pinning the exact sub-pixel value, since actualBoundingBox glyph metrics vary slightly across Arial builds/platforms (per the measure_element builder's field notes).
